item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"I won't have this baby… not without you."
"You can't leave. Stay."

I think these two lines should be flipped so the revelation is the last thing in the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this one. I have a bad habit of thinking of "Euclid" as assigned to anomalies that cause harm. That instinct is, of course, wrong, and it's refreshing to read an article that doesn't make me automatically go, "Why isn't this Safe? This should be Safe." +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hey, just letting you know you are the first SCP series 4 entry to ever be read on youtube! Eastside SCP read your SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: New slot and got round to making those edits I wanted to do. Thanks to all who upvoted it during the contest.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
On the one hand: I really, really like the concept, and I like the goal of just writing a short ghost story, and I think what she does is creepy without being over the top.

On the other hand: Not sure it does enough for me. The dialogue in particular feels too straightforward — it doesn't make her feel real to me.

Overall: Not sure what I think, even though I generally like it.

item.title: Re: Total honesty here
item.content:encoded: My first actual post and i botch a their VS there, Way to go me!

item.title: Total honesty here
item.content:encoded: Real nice skip

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1 for a good, solid, ghost story. I think this would be a totally appropriate SCP-3000. The theme was "horror" not "epic horror".

resulting into eventual hostile aggression against the victim where they are suddenly transferred under SCP-3000's grave site.

This seems really awkwardly phrased to me. She teleports them into her coffin?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I would like to see this reworked into a one off tale. I think the adherence to the SCP format is a massive detriment to any attempt at making this kind of thing frightening.
I realize that people read this site in different ways, some identifying as a foundation member, maybe other as an onlooker, who knows; but thats besides the point,
The point I'm trying to make is that this is too objective and not subjective. When you tell a ghost story you need to get the listeners to identify with the protagonists. If you are telling a ghost story around a camp fire then the story must take place at a campfire to get the listeners in. If you tell it indoors at during a rainstorm then you say its raining in the story. If the audience is a group of college students on spring break then so are the protagonists.
I guess the point I'm really trying to make is that this doesn't do enough to get the reader to identify with the the person in bed with the dead body and the better you get the reader to think of themselves as the person in the bed the more likely you are to get them to have a hard time falling asleep and isn't that was horror is really about?
This isn't to say this is the only way to get people into the story but this is a ghost story.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The messages are pretty simple and shallow, and ultimately weren't compelling enough for me to upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it. I feel like we suffer from a competitive atmosphere, where everyone is trying to go for the most obscure folklore, or the scariest take on a GoI, or the most improbable scientific anomaly. We need more of these, and I urge you to continue writing simple, Series I-style, to-the-point SCPs.

That said, in my own opinion, a SCP-X000 should be grandiose. This is not. While I would love to see this as SCP-3043 or what have you, it's just not epic enough to introduce a series. I'm sorry. No vote yet, will upvote after contest.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You wrote a dead woman who manifests in the middle of the night to spoon people. That's pretty objectively horrifying.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was gonna neutral vote, but then my brain provided me with this horrifying piece of information.

It's the corpse of a woman who's been dead since the 70s that teleports into your bed while you're asleep.

And then it touches your face.

Nope. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not actually sure why this is supposed to be scary, or where the "victim" part is. I think this would shoot to totally creepy levels if it emphasized her doing specific things to men who sleep alone while they're asleep. More things than laying by and saying vague things.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thought it was more sad than scary. I'll have to upvote this after the contest.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And this is officially the first 3000 entry to actually scare me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really dig this.

Its a simple ghost story, no extra bells and whistles to bog it down, and delivers a rather potent punch as the reader learns more about Ms Parks and her would be love.

+1 LurkD. Solid job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: She's giving 110% out there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Woops, good catch. Fixed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like this sweet little nugget of creepiness. A good ghost story every once in a while can't hurt, especially not this Foundation-y twist on it.

Excellent characterization, as befitting a fun, spooky tale. Great job Lurk.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have minor gripes about the grammar in Addendum-3000-001 but otherwise this is good. I have a hunch these contests won't be won by anything but horror epics though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In 78% of cases, SCP-3000 immediately departed upon viewing the photo. 32% have invoked a passive-aggressive conversation from SCP-3000

One of those percentages needs to change, unless there is an additional anomaly going on here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Simplistic, but that's the point. It's effective enough for me, and the horror of someone else on the bed can be something worth fearing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a little bit too simple for my taste, and the messages ultimately didn't really resonate with me that well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The ellipses are utterly terrible, but this was depressing as hell.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I do like this! It's not going for broke, it's just a sweet and simple ghost story about a sad and angry lover. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks ProcyonLotor, Decibelles, and Vezaz for helping me temper this.

I didn't want to write the next horror epic, this is just a short ghost story. Hope you like it.

I own the image used in this article and am using it under the CC license.
